description: Meta-lessons captured during the architecture-awareness initiative (priorities 1-11). Each lesson documents a recurring failure mode and the durable guardrail that catches it.

lessons.title: Silent rule-shape mismatch between caller and callee
lessons.summary: scripts/capture_arch_violation.py expected stdin envelopes with key `rule_id`, but the native architecture engine emits `rule`. The capture script silently dedup'd zero violations because the lookup keyed on a missing field. Caught only when the verification check explicitly listed written decisions and counted zero.

guardrail.description: Fuzz-style test that pipes both `{"rule": ...}` and `{"rule_id": ...}` shapes; assert capture script handles both. Closes the contract loop.

lessons.title: Explore-agent dead-code false positives on CLI-routed scripts
lessons.summary: build_acp.py / slice_acp.py were flagged dead by an exploratory dead-code scan, but they are CLI-routed (invoked via `python -m <module>` or argparse subcommands). Static grep for module-level imports misses these callers. Caught only by the orchestrator verifying live callers (`grep -r build_acp\|slice_acp scripts/ skills/`) before any move.

guardrail.description: Documents the CLI-routed-callers blind spot so the next dead-code sweep verifies callers via runtime invocation paths (subprocess, python -m, slash commands), not just static import grep.

lessons.title: Decision-store path cutover ambiguity (legacy vs new canonical)
lessons.summary: Decisions are stored under TWO paths: legacy `<repo>/.episodic/decisions/` and new `~/dev/git-folder/build-loop-memory/decisions/<project>/`. A verification check failed because the rule looked at the legacy path while the new canonical was authoritative. The memory facade (`scripts/memory_facade.py`) is the read API and fans out across both — bypassing it leads to phantom misses.

guardrail.description: Documents both decision-store paths (legacy + canonical) and names the facade as the only supported read API. Direct path access is fragile.
